**1 replication attempt of this paper has been recorded in the FORRT Library of Reproduction and Replication Attempts (FLoRA).**



> Yu et al. (2016). Wordn-+-2 preview effects in three-character Chinese idioms and phrases. Language, Cognition and Neuroscience, 31(9), 1130-1149. https://doi.org/10.1080/23273798.2016.1197954 [-](https://www.research.ed.ac.uk/en/publications/1320dcf0-9c15-49bb-af2d-8532a2f5e3cc)
> 
> **The authors reported the replication as:** "successful"
> 
> **Quote:** Experiment 1 replicated prior findings, although additional analyses of word n-+-1 and n-+-2 as a single region revealed significant preview effects regardless of word n-+-1 frequency.
> **Quote source:** "abstract"


Manual inspection may help to assess what led to the outcome described. Future research can learn from replications and build on them. More information about why and how we submitted this automated comment was submitted can be found [here](https://github.com/forrtproject/flora-pubpeer/blob/main/README.md)

> 
> **The authors reported the replication as:** "failed"
>
